subject: MUS
course_description: Introduction to basic methods and design of psychological research in the areas of learning, memory, sensation, perception, personality, and social processes. Laboratory exercises and fundamentals of scientific writing are emphasized.
course_notes: ("Introduction to research design and scientific thought in psychology, including observational, survey, and experimental approaches. Lectures and laboratory exercises provide students with a basis for (a) understanding and evaluating published research; (b) planning, executing, and reporting the results of research studies, and (c) communicating research findings clearly and concisely with APA style." The class lectures meet on Tuesdays and Thursdays. Students must also sign up for a 75-minute lab on Mondays. Course requirements include collaboration on an original empirical research project, written assignments in the service of a full-length APA-style research paper, and participation in a research poster session. A required course for psychology majors, PSYC200W should be taken during the sophomore or junior year. OF NOTE: Freshmen and seniors CANNOT take this course.") If the lecture section is full, please send Lorenza Houser (lhouser@emory.edu) the following information to be added on to the in-house waitlist: student ID, semester/year completed QTM 100, expected graduation, planning to study abroad (Y/N)-if Yes, semester/year, rank ALL lab sections according to priority (1st - last). NOTE: The department will decide on the waitlist a week or at the beginning of the spring semester.)
requisites: MUS 122 or equivalent transfer credit as prerequisite.
